description_long: This is a vintage slide projector, specifically a Diapol Automate, with a rectangular, boxy body finished in a light gray color. The front features a prominent circular lens assembly, slightly protruding from the surface, and a visible brand label. On one side, there is a blue slide tray with multiple slots for holding photographic slides, which is partially recessed into the body. The top of the projector is mostly flat with a small rectangular vent, while the rear includes additional venting and power connections. The base and some side elements are finished in a darker gray or black, giving the object a two-tone appearance.
description: A vintage Diapol Automate slide projector with a boxy gray body, circular lens, and blue slide tray. It features vents, a brand label, and a two-tone color scheme.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue slide tray with multiple slots and a rectangular vent on the top.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular lens, brand label, and the end of the slide tray.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain gray surface with a vented section at the bottom.
description_view_3: Back and side view showing the rear of the slide tray, power connections, and additional venting.
attribute_brand_logo: The brand logo 'Diapol Automate' is visible on the front right side of the projector, printed in gray and black text on a light gray background.
attribute_lens_type: fixed lens, circular, moderately sized, with a visible adjustment ring around the lens housing.
attribute_control_panel_design: A small control panel is visible on the rear side, featuring a few buttons and dials, likely for power and slide advancement, with minimal labeling.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Rectangular vent on the top and a horizontal slatted vent on the lower rear side, both with parallel slats for airflow.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
